points.text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS‑CoV‑2.
The disease began spreading worldwide in January 2020, leading to a pandemic.
The WHO declared COVID-19 a global health emergency in March 2020.
The WHO ended the global health emergency status in May 2023.
COVID-19 is an illness caused by the virus SARS‑CoV‑2, also known as severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007]
The disease began spreading at the end of 2019 and became a global pandemic in 2020 [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
SARS‑CoV‑2 spreads mainly between people in close contact, most commonly through tiny droplets in the air [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
Many infected individuals experience mild illness or no symptoms at all [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
Older adults and people with certain medical conditions are at higher risk of severe illness, hospitalization, or death [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]
